meaning: n.解构,拆析(文学评论用语,指找出文本中自身逻辑矛盾或自我拆解因素,从而摧毁文本在人们心目中的传统建构)
examples: This is a common response to deconstruction.
Deconstruction doesn't say texts have no meaning.
'My sculptures don't need any complex deconstruction;
About then, I suspect, we will also grow tired of irony, deconstruction and post-modernism.
Bush-appointed Ben Bernanke was already at the Fed in those years promoting that monetary deconstruction.
